TE Action Alert -- EFF 4/18


Notice Text:

In order to maintain the operational integrity of the system, TE is issuing an Action Alert pursuant to Section 4.3 of the General Terms and Conditions of TE's FERC Gas Tariff effective 9:00AM CCT April 18, 2026, to all OBA and Transportation Contracts, with the exception of those governed by a FERC gas tariff, in Texas Eastern's Market Area Zones M2-24, M2-30, and M3. 

 

This Action Alert does not affect the ability of TE to receive or deliver quantities of gas for scheduled nominations to any customer or pipeline.

 

This Action Alert will remain in effect until 9:00 AM CCT April 19, 2026.

 

Please refer to the table below for the effected TETCO zones, dates, and tolerance percentages.

Zone

Gas Day April 17

Gas Day April 18

Gas Day April 19 and until further notice

M1-24"

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

M1-30"

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

M2-24"

No Action Alert or OFO

Action Alert In Effect:

No Due Shipper  10%/500

No Action Alert or OFO

M2-30"

No Action Alert or OFO

Action Alert In Effect:

No Due Shipper  10%/500

No Action Alert or OFO

M3

No Action Alert or OFO

Action Alert In Effect:

No Due Shipper  10%/500

No Action Alert or OFO

ETX

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

STX

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

WLA

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

ELA

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

No Action Alert or OFO

 

NOTE:

Deliveries No DP : Shippers are not to take more than scheduled

Deliveries No DS : Shippers are not to take less than scheduled

Receipts No DP : Shippers are not to give less than scheduled

Receipts No DS : Shippers are not to give more than scheduled


The penalty for the Action Alert will be equal to 110% of the daily Platts Gas Daily "Daily Price Survey" posting for the High Common price for the geographical region, as defined in Section 8.5(a) of the General Terms and Conditions of TE's FERC Gas Tariff for the day on which such violation occurred.  In addition, TE will not permit retroactive nominations to avoid an Action Alert penalty except for shippers who can acquire day ending Due Pipe supply.

 

TE may be required to issue an hourly OFO pursuant to General Terms and Conditions Section 4.3(H) to impose further restrictions in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.

 

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.

 



BackPrint
